Cameras and premium audio devices

Amazon devices





Cashify has announced that its offline footprint has expanded to 95 cities with over 250 Cashify’s Branded outlets. The company has also introduced Cashify Select, a premium refurbished device offering, as customer demand shifts from entry-level devices to high-end smartphones. Mandeep Manocha, Co-Founder and CEO, Cashify said “India’s refurbished electronics market is evolving rapidly, and customer expectations are evolving with it. Over the last year, we have seen strong demand not only across more cities, but also across more categories and more premium products. Customers today want access to high-quality devices with greater assurance, transparency, and convenience, and that is what is shaping the next phase of growth for the category”.Cashify has also diversified its portfolio beyond smartphones and now offers laptops, wearables, audio devices, tablets, and gaming consoles, reflecting evolving customer demand for affordable, high-quality electronics. The industry is also witnessing early signs of repeat purchases and upgrade cycles across markets, indicating that India’s re-commerce ecosystem is maturing steadily, the company further added.As part of its next phase of growth, Cashify has launched Cashify Select, a premium layer within its refurbished offerings aimed at customers seeking top-tier quality, enhanced assurance, and a near-new experience. The offering includes:Nakul Kumar, Co-Founder and CMO, Cashify said “Refurbished electronics in India are moving from being purely price-driven to becoming increasingly value and trust-driven. Customers are more willing to spend on premium devices if they feel confident about the quality and reliability of what they are buying. Cashify Select is our response to that shift. It is designed for customers who want the assurance of quality, while still benefiting from the value that refurbished offers”.The company says that every device sold under Cashify Select undergoes a structured evaluation framework, including cosmetic grading, performance and battery health checks, verified component quality, and standardized multi-level testing. In addition to top-grade refurbished devices, the platform also offers open-box products, expanding choices for customers seeking premium devices at accessible price points.
Article Summary: Cashify has announced that its offline footprint has expanded to 95 cities with over 250 Cashify’s Branded outlets. The company has also introduced Cashify Select, a premium refurbished device offering. Cashify has diversified its portfolio beyond smartphones and now offers laptops, wearables, audio devices, tablets, and gaming consoles.

Article Title: Billionaire investor Mark Cuban: I was telling my daughter, you're always going to have a job if you ...
Article Text:
Mark Cuban: Professionals must reinvent themselves





Billionaire investor Mark Cuban recently shared a ‘critical’ piece of advice that he once gave to his daughter. During an appearance at Big Technology Podcast, Cuban said that if he was graduating right now, “or if I was a 16-year-old looking for a job, I would learn everything there is to know about AI”. Answering the question “Who succeeds with AI,” Cuban replied saying “Whoever learns how to use the (AI) tools the best”. “It's like I was telling my daughter, if you learn how to use these tools, and you know how to think critically, you're curious, so you're always learning, you're always going to have a job because AI doesn't know the consequences of its action,” he said.He further stated that one must not be in a business where “you just trust the AI and it's like”, adding “Oh what did it do”.“You need somebody as the buffer to understand how, where, when, and why to or why not to use it. And that's critical. And that's middle management in a lot of cases,” he stated.During the podcast, he also advised professionals to “reinvent themselves”, stating that by doing so, one can “make all these pieces work”.“And on top of that, you're going to have companies, like I said earlier, that have to completely reinvent themselves.And when you reinvent themselves, you're going to need people that understand how to make all these pieces work. And so I think there's going to be plenty of jobs,” he said.“If you’re not the person who knows how to do vibe coding or do all these different things with agents and Claude—whoever does is going to take your place,” Mark Cuban said.“You will always have an edge over everybody around you if you’re using AI to learn,” Cuban said. “If you’re just using it just so you don’t have to do the work and it’s your drunk intern, I mean, you’re going to struggle,” he noted.
Article Summary: Billionaire investor Mark Cuban recently shared a ‘critical’ piece of advice that he once gave to his daughter. Cuban said that if he was graduating right now, “or if I was a 16-year-old looking for a job, I would learn everything there is to know about AI”

Article Title: Anthropic may soon form $1.5 billion AI venture with Wall Street firms
Article Text:
A new kind of consulting firm





Anthropic is reportedly finalising a massive $1.5 billion joint venture with some of the biggest names on Wall Street. The deal aims to create a new entity that will help businesses, specifically those owned by private equity firms, learn how to use AI to work faster and save money. Citing people familiar with the matter, The Wall Street Journal reported that the official announcement could come on late Monday (IST). This move signals an escalation in the race to bring AI into the corporate world, where Anthropic currently holds a strong lead.The report claims that the venture is being led by prominent leaders of the financial world, including Anthropic, Blackstone and Hellman & Friedman are expected to lead the investment, each putting in roughly $300 million.Goldman Sachs is set to join with an investment of about $150 million with additional partners include General Atlantic and several other firms to reach the total $1.5 billion commitment.Rather than just selling software, this new company will act as a consulting arm, the report said, adding that it will focus on teaching businesses how to actually weave Anthropic’s AI tools into their day-to-day operations.The primary targets are “portfolio companies”, which are businesses owned by private equity firms.Since these companies are already under pressure to cut costs and improve efficiency, they are seen as the perfect customers for advanced AI tools. Anthropic isn't the only one eyeing Wall Street. Rival OpenAI has also been in talks to form its own joint venture with private equity firms to spread its technology, the report pointed out.While OpenAI is a household name, Anthropic is widely considered the current leader when it comes to selling AI to businesses. Anthropic’s revenue has recently exploded, largely due to the success of Claude Code, its popular tool for computer programmers.Moreover, there has been positive reports around the prowess of Anthropic’s Mythos AI model that can find loopholes in software that the companies never knew existed. This $1.5 billion deal comes at a time for Anthropic, which is reportedly considering going public as soon as this year.
Article Summary: Anthropic is reportedly finalising a massive $1.5 billion joint venture with some of the biggest names on Wall Street. The deal aims to create a new entity that will help businesses learn how to use AI to work faster and save money.

What Bill Gates’ quote really means

Everyone is unique. People have different qualities, backgrounds, and timeframes for success. Self-respect above all else: By comparing yourself to another person, you’re undermining your own potential and accomplishments.

By comparing yourself to another person, you’re undermining your own potential and accomplishments. Measure progress based on personal development: Growth should always be assessed relative to one’s former self.

Why this idea resonates even today



Building confidence without comparison

Article Title: 6 Google Gemini AI prompts to recreate your childhood self-portraits: Easy step-by-step guide and quick tips
Article Text:
AI Generated

Gemini AI prompts to create your 'childhood self' portraits



“A black and white cinematic portrait of a woman facing her childhood self across a table with a birthday cake in the center. The cake has candles forming the number ‘25’. The child is labeled ‘2001’ above her, and the adult is labeled ‘2026’. Both look at each other with soft emotional smiles. Studio lighting, shallow depth of field, emotional storytelling, fine art photography style, minimal background, highly detailed skin texture.”

“A creative conceptual portrait where a grown woman looks into a mirror and sees her childhood self reflected. The reflection shows ‘1999’ while the real person is labeled ‘2026’. Emotional, symbolic composition, soft studio lighting, minimal modern interior, fine art photography style with surreal realism.”

“A split-frame cinematic portrait showing two timelines: standing and holding each others' hand. On the right, a child labeled ‘2001’ looking forward; on the left, the same person as an adult labeled ‘2026’ looking back. Black and white aesthetic, dramatic lighting, symmetrical composition, ultra-detailed fine art photography.”

“A close-up emotional black and white portrait of a woman gently smiling while looking at her childhood self across a sofa. The child is labeled ‘2001’ and the adult ‘2026’. Focus on facial expressions, soft cinematic lighting, shallow depth of field, realistic skin texture, intimate emotional storytelling style.”

“A high-end editorial studio portrait of a woman and her childhood self standing and look at each other. The child is labeled ‘2001’ and the adult ‘2026’. Clean grey background, softbox lighting, symmetrical composition, fashion photography style, ultra-sharp detail, elegant and modern emotional concept.”

“A top-down cinematic composition showing a woman and her childhood self seated across a round table. In the center is a birthday cake with ‘25’ candles. The child is labeled ‘2001’ and the adult ‘2026’. Objects like old toys and modern items subtly placed on each side. Soft neutral lighting, minimalist aesthetic, fine art photography style.”

How to create your childhood self : Step-by-step guide



Step 1: Define the core childhood version

Step 2: Write a detailed base prompt

Step 3: Add memory-based details

Step 4: Generate and review output

Step 5: Refine through iteration

Quick practical tips for better results



Tip 1: Focus on emotions first

Tip 2: Use consistent memory anchors

Tip 3: Keep details simple

Tip 4: Blend imagination with memory

Tip 5: Focus on personal storytelling





Going back to one’s childhood self through recreation is a very creative and imaginative experience that combines memory, visual imagination, and storytelling. It enables the individual to go back to another version of themselves and transform his/her memories into an artistic expression. Recreation is often employed in the creation of digital art, in photography, as well as in journal entries, where emotional remembrance is critical. The recreation involves focusing on facial expressions, clothing, environment, and little behaviors when one was still young. In doing so, the recreation does not rely entirely on the accurate remembrance of what happened, but on the emotional essence of the event, which is then transformed into a narrative with the aid of visual techniques, often adding depth, nostalgia, personal meaning, emotional connection, and a stronger sense of identity and reflection.The creation of the childhood self-portrait starts by organising one’s memory in an organised fashion. Here, the emphasis lies in finding the features from childhood that include hairstyle, facial expression, clothes, surroundings, and many more. Such features are not regarded as historical data, but rather feelings that have been stored within one’s mind over the years. The method will work effectively if one remembers such things spontaneously and turns them into imagery without being too particular.The process begins by deciding the exact age range you want to recreate, such as 5, 8, or 12 years old. This helps Gemini structure facial proportions, body type, and expression accurately. The age selection also influences clothing style and environmental context in the output.A structured prompt must be prepared before inputting into Gemini. The description should include physical traits, emotional expression, and environment. The clarity of description directly impacts output quality.Gemini produces stronger results when personal memory cues are included. These may involve school uniforms, favourite toys, room settings, or seasonal details. The inclusion of such elements helps the AI construct a more personalised visual identity rather than a generic child portrait.After submitting the prompt, the AI generates the first version. This stage involves reviewing facial accuracy, emotional tone, and background consistency. If the result does not align with expectations, adjustments to the prompt are required instead of starting from scratch.Gemini allows repeated refinement. Small changes in wording such as lighting, expression, or clothing style should be applied gradually. This step improves realism and emotional depth in the final image.The emotional tone of childhood memories often creates a more authentic result than precise physical accuracy. Expression and mood should guide the creation process.Certain repeated memories, such as school uniforms or favourite toys, help maintain stability in the visual reconstruction.Overloading the image with excessive elements can dilute the impact. Simplicity allows the core memory to stand out clearly.Not all childhood details are fully remembered, so filling gaps with thoughtful imagination helps complete the visual story.The final result works best when it reflects a personal narrative rather than a generic childhood representation.
Article Summary: Gemini AI prompts to create your 'childhood self' portraits. The process begins by organising one’s memory in an organised fashion. The recreation involves focusing on facial expressions, clothing, environment, and little behaviors when one was still young.

Article Title: AC fires in summer: Likely reasons, warning signs, and safety tips for AC users
Article Text:
Keep Your Air Conditioner Clean and Safe with these simple steps



Common reasons behind AC fires

Dirty air filters

Blocked vents

Electrical issues

Incorrect temperature settings

Improper AC size

Poor insulation

Warnings signs before AC fire that one must not avoid

Burning smell or smoke: This may indicate overheating or electrical faults

Unusual noises: Loose or damaged parts may cause strange sounds

Frequent on-off cycles: This can signal internal issues

Weak or uneven cooling: May point to blocked airflow or system problems

Split vs Window AC: What’s different and why safety practices vary

Window ACs need better airflow and cooling around the unit itself

Split ACs need proper installation, wiring checks, and outdoor unit care

Safety tips for window AC users

Ensure proper installation: The unit should be securely fixed and slightly tilted outward for drainage

Avoid blocking airflow: Keep both front and back sides clear for proper heat release

Clean filters frequently: Dirty filters can block airflow and cause overheating

Use a dedicated power source: Avoid extension cords and ensure stable wiring

Check for overheating signs: If the unit feels too hot or smells burnt, switch it off immediately

Keep surroundings dust-free: Dust buildup can affect internal parts and increase fire risk

Role of installation in preventing AC fires

Tips to optimise cooling performance of your split AC



Tip 1: Set the Right Temperature (24°C–26°C)

Tip 2: Clean Your AC Filters Regularly

Tip 3: Use Smart Features (Sleep Mode, Timers, Eco Mode)

Sleep Mode: Automatically adjusts the temperature during the night. It prevents overcooling and saves power while you sleep.

Timer: Lets you set the AC to turn off automatically after a few hours. This is ideal if you tend to fall asleep with the AC on.

Eco Mode: Maintains moderate cooling with reduced compressor usage. It uses less power and is perfect for mild summer days.

Tip 4: Keep Your Room Sealed & Shaded

Use thick curtains or blackout blinds to block sunlight during the day.

Seal any gaps in doors and windows using weather stripping or draft stoppers.

Tip 5: Schedule Regular Maintenance

What should your annual AC check-up include?

Gas level inspection

Condenser coil cleaning

Fan and blower check

Thermostat calibration

Electrical connections check

When to schedule AC maintenance?

Bonus Tips to Maximise Cooling and Savings





Use Ceiling Fans Alongside AC

Avoid Heat-Generating Appliances During the Day

Use Energy Star-rated ACs

How to clean AC air filters

Slide the filter off of the unit.

Use a soft brush or vacuum cleaner to remove any dust or debris on the filter.

Soak the filter in a solution of water and mild detergent for 30 minutes.

Rinse the filter and let it air dry in a well-ventilated area that is out of direct sunlight.

Reinstall the filter.

Conclusion





As summer heat rises across the country, reports of air conditioner (AC) fires and related incidents are also increasing, raising concerns about safety in homes and offices. Experts say that most of these incidents are linked to poor maintenance, overuse, or electrical faults that build up over time. With ACs running for long hours during peak heat, the risk of overheating and system failure becomes higher. Regular upkeep and basic precautions can help reduce these risks. Understanding the causes, early warning signs, and simple safety steps can help users keep their cooling systems running safely during the hot months.Why do AC fires happen more often in the summer? AC units have to work harder when it's really hot outside, and they often run for long periods of time without stopping.This constant use puts a lot of stress on parts like compressors, wiring, and filters. If the system isn't properly maintained, this stress can cause overheating or electrical problems, which can lead to fires.Dust, blocked airflow, and poor ventilation can further increase the risk. In many cases, small issues that go unnoticed over time turn into bigger problems during peak summer usage.Summer’s peaking and your AC needs service. As you’re home, and our service teams may not be able to reach you right now because of lockdown restrictions, here are useful tips that will help you get your AC in good shape before we are allowed to visit you in person.Several factors can lead to AC malfunctions and fire risks. Some of them include:Dust and dirt can block airflow, forcing the AC to work harder.This can cause overheating and increase fire risk.If vents are covered by furniture or curtains, airflow is restricted. This leads to uneven cooling and puts extra pressure on the system.Loose wiring, faulty connections, or poor installation can trigger short circuits or overheating.Setting the AC to very low temperatures for long periods can strain the system and lead to overheating.An AC that is too large or too small for a room may switch on and off frequently, increasing wear and tear.If cool air escapes easily, the AC has to work harder, which can lead to overheating over time.AC systems often show early signs before a major failure. Experts say users should watch out for:Ignoring these signs can increase the risk of serious damage or fire.Window ACs and split ACs work differently, and this affects how safety risks show up in each system. A window AC is a single unit that holds all of its parts, including the compressor, condenser, and cooling system. Because of this compact design, heat builds up faster if airflow is blocked or maintenance is poor. The electrical load is also concentrated in one place, which can make it more likely to overheat.A split AC, on the other hand, has two separate parts: an indoor unit and an outdoor compressor. This setup makes it easier to control heat inside, but it also makes it more likely that there will be problems like bad wiring between the units, refrigerant leaks, and poor ventilation of the outdoor unit.Because of these differences:Window ACs require close attention to airflow, installation, and electrical safety:Correct installation plays a key role in reducing the risk of AC fires. Even a good-quality air conditioner can become unsafe if it is not installed properly. Many fire incidents are linked to issues such as poor wiring, wrong placement, or lack of ventilation — all of which often start at the installation stage. One of the most important factors is electrical safety. ACs draw high power, so they must be connected to a proper circuit with the right wiring, earthing, and a dedicated power supply. Loose connections, low-quality wires, or shared sockets can lead to overheating and short circuits. Placement of the unit is also critical.For window ACs, improper fitting or blocked rear ventilation can trap heat inside the unit.For split ACs, placing the outdoor unit in a closed or poorly ventilated space can cause the compressor to overheat.Another key aspect is correct sizing and load calculation. If an AC is too small for a room, it will overwork continuously. If it is too large, it may switch on and off frequently. Both situations increase stress on the system and raise the risk of failure.Improper installation can also affect drainage and airflow. Water leakage or blocked airflow can damage internal parts and increase the chances of electrical faults. Experts say installation should always be done by trained technicians who follow safety standards. A well-installed AC not only performs better but also reduces the risk of overheating, electrical issues, and fire hazards.Consumer electronics manufacturer Voltas has listed 5 different tips that a user can follow to optimise cooling performance of your AC. These are:The first step in saving energy is simple, set your AC temperature between 24°C and 26°C. This is the most energy-efficient range.Running the AC at very low temperatures forces the compressor to work harder. That leads to higher power usage and more wear on the machine. According to government recommendations, 24°C is the ideal setting for both comfort and efficiency. Pairing this temperature range with a 1.5 ton 5 star AC ensures maximum savings and optimal cooling even during peak summer hours.Pro tip: Each degree lower than 24°C can increase power consumption by 6%. Staying within the 24°C–26°C range keeps both your room and electricity bill cool.Dusty filters block airflow. They force your AC to work harder and reduce cooling efficiency. A clogged filter can increase energy use by up to 15%. To avoid this, clean your filters once every two weeks during peak summer months. If you live in a dust-prone area, check them weekly.Cleaning is simple. Just remove the filters, rinse them under cold water, and let them dry before reattaching. Clean filters ensure smooth airflow and faster cooling.Modern air conditioners come with built-in smart features. These are not just convenient, but they save energy too.Make sure you use these features. They’re easy to operate and help cut down on unnecessary energy use.If your room is not sealed properly, your AC will have to cool hot air entering from outside. That leads to wasted energy.Here’s what to do:This reduces heat gain and keeps the cold air inside. A well-insulated room cools faster and stays cool longer.Your AC needs yearly servicing to perform at its best. Without regular maintenance, parts wear out and energy usage goes up.Right before the summer begins. A pre-season check ensures the AC runs smoothly through the hottest months without sudden breakdowns. Proper servicing can improve efficiency by 10%–15% and extend your AC’s life by several years.Voltas has also listed bonus tips:Fans help distribute the cold air evenly across the room. This allows you to raise the AC temperature by 1–2 degrees without feeling any difference. Using both together reduces the pressure on your air conditioner and cuts down electricity use.Microwaves, ovens, irons, and other appliances release heat. Using them during the hottest part of the day makes your AC work harder.Best time to use them? Early mornings or late evenings. Also, switch off lights when not needed, they generate heat too.If your AC is over 8–10 years old, it might be time to upgrade. Newer models with a BEE 5-star rating or Energy Star label use far less power. Voltas offers a wide range of energy-efficient 5 star AC options, perfect for Indian summers and long-term savings.While the initial cost may be high, the savings on electricity bills make up for it in the long run. These models are designed for efficient cooling and longer life. For small rooms or compact spaces, consider switching to a 1 ton 5 star AC, which offers great cooling with reduced electricity usage.It is generally advised to clean the air filter every two weeks or when the CF (filter-cleaning reminder) appears on the indoor unit display (if your AC supports the feature). As per a Samsung helppage, the time between cleanings may vary, depending on the usage and environmental conditions. Here’s how to clean AC filtersCooling your home in summer doesn't have to cost a fortune. With a few smart adjustments, you can cut your electricity bills and improve your AC's performance.Set the right temperature. Clean your filters. Use smart modes. Keep your room sealed. Schedule timely maintenance. And don’t forget the bonus tips, use fans, avoid extra heat, and consider upgrading your AC if it’s outdated. Small changes can lead to big savings. A well-maintained, efficiently used AC not only lasts longer but also keeps you cool without breaking the bank.
Article Summary: Air conditioner fires and related incidents are increasing in the UK. Most incidents are linked to poor maintenance, overuse, or electrical faults. Air conditioners have to work harder when it's hot outside. This puts a lot of stress on parts, wiring, and filters. If the system isn't properly maintained, stress can lead to fires.

Article Title: Government clarifies on reports of new Aadhaar card design, says ...
Article Text:
No design change coming to Aadhaar card



Aadhaar’s current status







The government of India has now officially denied reports suggesting that a new design for Aadhaar cards is being introduced, calling such claims “unwanted confusion”. According to a statement issued by Ministry of electronics and IT, no changes are planned to the existing Aadhaar card format. This calcification cam after social media posts and online report claimed that a redesigned Aadhaar card was being rolled out. The government also empathised that these claims are false and misleading, urging the citizens to not fall for any misinformation.“There are intermittent news reports and social media posts explaining how Aadhaar’s look may change by the end of this year to just a photo and a QR code, alone. This is NOT CORRECT. There is no plan for any such changes. Such news reports and social media posts are creating unwanted confusion in the minds of the people. People in general are advised to ignore such reports and social media posts, and refer to official communication from UIDAI through its official social media handles and press releases issued through PIB.Media is also advised not to encourage such information,” reads the statement issued by Ministry of electronics and IT.The Aadhaar card, issued by the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI), remains one of the most widely used identity documents in the country. It is accepted across government and private services for verification, subsidies, and financial transactions. Officials reiterated that the existing design continues to be valid and functional, with no plans for replacement.The government has also advised citizens to rely only on official communication from UIDAI and PIB for updates related to Aadhaar. Any information circulating outside these channels should be treated with caution.
Article Summary: The government of India has officially denied reports suggesting that a new design for Aadhaar cards is being introduced. According to a statement issued by Ministry of electronics and IT, no changes are planned to the existing Aadhaar card format. Officials reiterated that the existing design continues to be valid and functional, with no plans for replacement.

Article Title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella makes a promise to Windows users; says: We will win back fans as we are doing ...
Article Text:
Xbox and other services also in focus

Windows remains a dominant platform





Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella recently said that the company is working to improve its consumer products, including Windows, as part of a broader effort to strengthen user engagement. Speaking during the company’s recent earnings call, Nadella said Microsoft is focusing on improving quality and addressing core user needs across its platforms. “When it comes to our consumer business, we are doing the foundational work required to win back fans and strengthen engagement across Windows, Xbox, Bing, and Edge,” Nadella said. “In the near term, we are focused on fundamentals, prioritizing quality and serving our core users better.”He also highlighted recent updates to Windows, saying: “With Windows, we recently announced performance improvements for lower memory devices, streamlined the Windows Update experience, and brought back focus to core features and fundamentals that matter most to our customers.”The comments come as the company looks to respond to feedback around Windows 11 and enhance user experience across its ecosystem.Satya Nadella said Microsoft is taking a similar approach with its gaming business. He noted that the company is “recommitting to our core fans and players” and pointed to recent changes in Game Pass as an example of responding to user feedback."With Windows, we recently announced performance improvements for lower memory devices, streamlined the Windows Update experience, and brought back focus to core features and fundamentals that matter most to our customers,” Nadella said adding “And you also see this in Xbox, where the team is recommitting to our core fans and players and shaping the future of play.Last week’s Game Pass changes are one example of how we are staying responsive to customer feedback. ”The company’s efforts come as it aims to improve engagement across multiple platforms, including Windows, Xbox and its online services.Microsoft also shared that Windows continues to have a large global presence. The company said active Windows devices now exceed 1.6 billion worldwide, underlining its position in the desktop market.“Monthly active Windows devices surpassed 1.6 billion, and over time Windows value will extend to deliver unmetered intelligence at the edge,” Nadella said, adding that Xbox active users have also increased. "We set new records for monthly Xbox active users in the quarter, as well as game streaming hours," Satya Nadella said.
Article Summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said the company is working to improve its consumer products, including Windows. He said Microsoft is focusing on improving quality and addressing core user needs across its platforms. Comments come as the company looks to respond to feedback around Windows 11 and enhance user experience across its ecosystem.

Article Title: Samsung appoints Lee Won-jin to lead TV unit as market competition heats up
Article Text:
Representative image

Samsung TV faces growing competition







Samsung Electronics has appointed Lee Won-jin as its TV head. In a statement, Samsung announced that Lee Won-jin, who was previously head of the Global Marketing Office, is ⁠the new head of its Visual Display Business. Won-jin will succeed Yong Seok-woo, who after stepping down from the current role will serve as an adviser. The appointment comes for the first time in more ‌than two years, as the company is reportedly facing increased competition from Chinese rivals at home and abroad. Last month, Samsung revealed that its TV profit declined in the first quarter due to staggering demand and escalating raw-material costs.A Reuters report quotes a Samsung Electronics official who told the agency that the new leader is expected to bring a fresh perspective and the change needed for the TV ‌business, ⁠which is facing intensifying market competition.As mentioned above, Samsung is facing competition from Chinese rivals in the TV and home appliance category. As per Reuters report, China's TCL Electronics and Japan's Sony signed binding agreements in March this year for a strategic partnership in the home entertainment field, increasing pressure on rivals.As per a previous Nikkei report, the company is considering discontinuing sales of home appliances and TVs in China within this year in the face of competition from ⁠Chinese companies that have undercut rivals.The Nikkei reported that Samsung's Chinese rivals have improved their quality on top of their already low prices. Additionally, the growing preference for domestic brands among Chinese consumers is adding to the challenge that foreign competitors face. “Of the 32.89 million TVs that were shipped in China in 2025, Samsung and other foreign brands made up fewer than 1 million units, according to Chinese research firm Runto Technology,” the report said.“The company is expected to make a final decision as soon as the end of April and begin explaining the move to its Chinese employees and business partners,” the report said, further adding “It would gradually dispose of its inventory in China, aiming to end sales this year.”
Article Summary: Lee Won-jin will succeed Yong Seok-woo, who after stepping down from the current role will serve as an adviser. Samsung is reportedly facing increased competition from Chinese rivals at home and abroad. Last month, Samsung revealed that its TV profit declined in the first quarter due to staggering demand and escalating raw-material costs.

Article Title: GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen seeks to buy eBay for $56 billion in cash and stock, says ‘It could …’
Article Text:
GameStop offers to buy eBay: Details and stake build-up

GameStop CEO’s vision to build eBay as Amazon competitor

Challenges GameStop may face in acquiring eBay

Poll Should GameStop focus on improving its own business instead of acquiring eBay? Yes No





GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen has made an “unsolicited” offer to acquire eBay for about $56 billion, according to a report by The Wall Street Journal. The report said Cohen believes there is a strong opportunity to turn eBay into a much bigger competitor to Amazon. As per the report, the offer includes a mix of cash and stock and comes as GameStop builds a stake in eBay. Cohen also said he is prepared to take the proposal directly to shareholders if the company does not respond positively. “EBay should be worth—and will be worth—a lot more money,” Cohen told the publication. “I’m thinking about turning eBay into something worth hundreds of billions of dollars.”He added that combining GameStop and eBay could help reduce costs and improve earnings.Both companies already have some overlap, especially in areas like collectibles and trading cards. “There is nobody who is more qualified, based on my experience, to run the eBay business,” Cohen said, referring to his work at GameStop and Chewy.According to the report, GameStop has built about a 5% stake in eBay and is offering $125 per share. This represents a roughly 20% premium over eBay’s previous closing price.Cohen said GameStop has secured a commitment letter from TD Bank for up to $20 billion in debt financing to support the deal. The company also has around $9 billion in cash, though it is not clear how the full amount for the acquisition will be arranged.GameStop has formally sent an offer letter to eBay’s chairman, outlining the proposal and its plans.Cohen said GameStop’s physical stores could be used to support eBay’s online marketplace. For example, stores could help collect and verify items sold by eBay users. He also suggested that eBay should expand into live commerce, where products are sold through real-time video streams.“It could be a legit competitor to Amazon,” Cohen said about eBay.According to the WSJ report, the deal would be difficult to complete, as GameStop is much smaller than eBay. GameStop is valued at around $12 billion, while eBay is valued at about $46 billion.Some analysts have raised concerns about the plan. They noted that eBay has recently reported strong results and is already improving its business.“Why disrupt things? The turnaround is working,” analysts said in a note cited in the report.
Article Summary: GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen has made an “unsolicited” offer to acquire eBay for about $56 billion. The offer includes a mix of cash and stock and comes as GameStop builds a stake in eBay. “I’m thinking about turning eBay into something worth hundreds of billions of dollars,” Cohen said.

Article Title: Greg Abel, CEO of world's richest investment company Berkshire Hathaway, to shareholders at AGM: Yes, AI offers productivity gains, but it in no way can replace humans as ...
Article Text:
Warren Buffet attended AGM sitting in the front row

Greg Abel makes a 'promise' to investors

Poll Should Berkshire Hathaway invest in AI for its subsidiaries? Yes, to stay competitive No, it's too risky Only with proven benefits





Berkshire Hathaway had its first annual shareholders meeting (AGM) in Omaha, Nebraska, four months after succeeding arguably the world's most famous investor -- Warren Buffet -- as chief executive officer. Organisers opened the meeting with an AI-generated video of Buffett, which Abel called a serious risk Berkshire manages every day. However, when it comes to investment in a world enamoured by AI companies, AI models and AI stocks; Berkshire Hathaway seemingly wants to follow a different path.Greg Abel told shareholders that the conglomerate will adopt artificial intelligence (AI) only where it adds clear value, rejecting the industry-wide hype in his first annual meeting as the designated successor to Warren Buffett . He said that AI must improve efficiency, safety, or decision-making before Berkshire deploys it.To aid this quest for continuous improvement, Abel outlined the technology transformation underway across Berkshire. He said that Berkshire is moving from being a buyer of technology to a builder of technology solutions for its businesses’ needs. "We're not going to do AI for the sake of AI," Abel said, adding that the tech has to be "additive to our businesses." Berkshire's new boss said the conglomerate's subsidiaries would employ AI prudently, in areas where it creates genuine value.According to analysts, Abel's views on AI largely extend Buffett's long-standing skepticism of unproven tech narratives, and stands in contrast to peers cutting jobs or rebranding around AI capabilities.Berkshire Hathaway's insurance head Ajit Jain too echoed similar views on using AI applications in the company's insurance businesses. Jain said that he views AI primarily as a tool for routine tasks and cost reduction but remains skeptical if it will replace human judgment in critical areas like pricing, claims settlement, or asset management. He pointed to railroad subsidiary BNSF, where targeted AI tools are sharpening operations, and to insurance, where the company uses technology to flag fraud and deepfake threats.Though Berkshire Chairman Warren Buffett was absent from the main stage, he still spoke at the meeting, delivering a short address to shareholders from his seat in the first row among Berkshire directors that focused on corporate leadership and, more specifically, successful transitions of power. Buffett, who remains Berkshire's chairman, assured the audience that "Greg is doing everything I did and then some," almost repeating the comments he made last year when he announced his retirement as CEO.The 95-year-old also praised Apple, one of Berkshire's most successful investments ever, and its departing CEO Tim Cook. In fact, taking the example of Apple Inc, Buffett tried to convince investors in Greg Abel's ability as Berkshire Hathaway CEO. He reminded the crowd that virtually no one knew who could successfully lead the iPhone maker after the death of Steve Jobs, and few investors knew who Tim Cook was at the time. Buffett, who invested $35 billion in Apple a decade ago, or about 10% of Berkshire’s resources, said that bet has since turned into $185 billion including dividends.Abel also assured shareholders he would not break up Berkshire, saying that it operated effectively and its bench of expertise was strong. "We want Berkshire to endure," he said. Abel also said that he is constantly evaluating opportunities to add to Berkshire's existing portfolio, whether that is acquiring public or private companies or a piece of a company.
Article Summary: Greg Abel told shareholders that the conglomerate will adopt artificial intelligence (AI) only where it adds clear value. Abel said that AI must improve efficiency, safety, or decision-making before Berkshire deploys it. Berkshire Chairman Warren Buffett was absent from the main stage, but still spoke at the meeting.

Article Title: How new Apple CEO John Ternus’s approach to cash management may lean closer to Steve Jobs’ than Tim Cook’s
Article Text:
John Ternus

A departure from the Cook Era

Ending the ‘net cash neutral’ policy

Poll Should Apple prioritize R&D over shareholder returns? Yes No





As Apple prepares for a leadership change, there are official hints that incoming CEO John Ternus may handle the company’s massive cash pile very differently than Tim Cook, and his approach to cash management may lean closer to Steve Jobs’ philosophy. According to a report by Bloomberg, while Cook turned Apple into a shareholder’s dream by returning more than $1 trillion through buybacks and dividends, Ternus appears to pivot back toward a flexible and aggressive investment.Mark Gurman reports that when Tim Cook took over nearly 15 years ago, he famously reversed Steve Jobs’ conservative stance on cash. Jobs, having steered Apple through a near-bankruptcy in the 1990s, preferred to keep cash on hand for product development.Cook, however, focused on being “shareholder-friendly,” launching a massive program of stock repurchases and dividends that helped Apple expand its investors’ pool and become a $4 trillion company.Now, with Ternus set to take the reins, that philosophy is said to be shifting. The report says that engineers and designers at Apple have long argued that the company’s billions would be better spent on major acquisitions, research and development (R&D) and hiring top talent.The clearest sign of this shift came during Apple’s recent quarterly call. The company announced it is abandoning its “net cash neutral” policy, a framework used since 2018 to keep its cash and debt in balance by aggressively spending down its money pile.“As we move ahead, we are no longer providing net cash neutral as a formal target, and we will independently evaluate cash and debt. Capital returns will continue to be important to our overall approach by delivering long-term shareholder value,” CFO Kevan Parekh Parekh said.This may mean that Apple would want to pursue “blockbuster” acquisitions or massive investments in AI infrastructure to keep pace with Silicon Valley rivals. Importantly, while buybacks won’t disappear, they may become less frequent or smaller as the company prioritises “necessary investments” to support the business.Unlike the operations-focused Tim Cook, John Ternus rose through the ranks on the product side of the business. This background likely influences his desire to have the financial “optionality” to fund a new wave of AI-powered devices and the infrastructure they require.Last week, Gurman reported that Apple has a pipeline of 10 products under Ternus – which is more than Cook’s under whom the company launched three new ones. Despite the policy shift, Ternus signaled that he isn't abandoning the discipline of his predecessor. In his first public comments as the CEO-in-waiting, he promised to manage finances with the same “thoughtfulness and discipline” as Cook.
Article Summary: Incoming CEO John Ternus may lean closer to Steve Jobs' philosophy. Cook turned Apple into a shareholder’s dream by returning more than $1 trillion through buybacks and dividends. Engineers and designers at Apple have long argued that the company's billions would be better spent on major acquisitions, research and development.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Jeff Bezos: "I want much of my philanthropic activity to be helping people in the here and now—short term—at the intersection of urgent need and lasting impact."
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Jeff Bezos

Quote of the day by Jeff Bezos

"I want much of my philanthropic activity to be helping people in the here and now—short term—at the intersection of urgent need and lasting impact."

Understanding the meaning behind Jeff Bezos’ quote

A shift from traditional long-term philanthropy

How this philosophy is reflected in modern philanthropy

Jeff Bezos and his philanthropic initiatives

Why addressing urgent needs matters

The importance of creating lasting impact

The intersection of urgency and sustainability

How individuals can apply this principle in daily life

Other famous quotes by Jeff Bezos

"When you look at our financial results, what you’re actually seeing are the long-run cumulative results of invention."

"If you want to be inventive, you have to be willing to fail."

"I think you can be stubborn on your vision but flexible on the details of how you get there."

"We didn’t ascend from our hunter-gatherer days by being satisfied. People have a voracious appetite for a better way."

"We do crazy things together and then make them normal."

The growing importance of strategic philanthropy

A balanced approach to solving global challenges

A clear message for meaningful action

Poll What do you think should be prioritized in charitable initiatives? Urgent needs Lasting impact Balancing both approaches





Many people think of philanthropy as a long-term effort to solve big problems in the world over many years. But Jeff Bezos 's quote gives a slightly different and more realistic view. It shows how important it is to meet immediate needs while also thinking about long-term effects. In short, it says that helping people today doesn't have to mean that things will stay the same in the future. Instead, both can live together. In a world where problems like poverty, climate change, and health emergencies need quick action, this idea is becoming more and more important. Long-term solutions are still very important, though. The quote shows a balanced approach, where quick action and careful planning go hand in hand.This makes giving money both helpful and responsive.The quote talks about two important ideas: "urgent need" and "lasting impact." These phrases may sound simple, but they show a big change in how people think about giving money to charity.Urgent need means that something needs to be done right away. This could mean getting help after a disaster, getting food, medical care, or a place to stay. These are problems where waiting can have big effects.On the other hand, lasting impact means coming up with solutions that keep helping people even after the first help is given.The quote says that good philanthropy should do two things: act quickly and plan for the future. It promotes a way of doing things where short-term actions are planned to help long-term growth.In the past, a lot of charitable work has been focused on long-term goals like improving education, building infrastructure, or doing scientific research. These areas are still important, but it can take years for changes to be seen.The method mentioned in this quote makes things happen more quickly. It knows that long-term plans are important, but people who need help right now can't wait years to get it. This change shows that more people are realising that both short-term and long-term goals are equally important.For example, giving people medical care during a crisis meets their immediate needs. Putting money into healthcare systems at the same time makes sure we are better prepared for the future. This quote brings this balance to light.Giving has become more data-driven and responsive in the last few years. A lot of groups now focus on finding places where quick action can also lead to long-term results.You can see this approach in programs that combine aid with development. Food aid programs are now often linked with agricultural support, which helps communities become self-sufficient over time. Education programs can give teachers things they need right away, like books and digital tools. They can also spend money on training teachers to make things better in the long run.The goal is to connect short-term help with long-term solutions in a way that makes sense, not to treat them as two different things.Jeff Bezos has done a lot of good things for others because of this way of thinking. One of the most important climate change projects is the Bezos Earth Fund. Many people think of climate action as something that will happen in the future, but the fund also helps projects that deal with environmental issues right now.Another example is the Day One Fund. It helps families who are homeless and supports education for young children. These programs are meant to help people right away and also make systems that will make their lives better in the long run.These actions show that the idea of setting both short-term and long-term goals can work in real life.Some problems need to be fixed right away in many parts of the world. Natural disasters, health problems, and economic problems can all quickly change people's lives. In these kinds of situations, moving quickly can make a big difference.When communities meet their urgent needs, they can stay stable and start rebuilding. People or groups can't get much out of long-term development programs without this initial help.That's why the quote says you should "act in the here and now." It knows that acting right away is not only helpful, but often necessary.It's important to get help right away, but it's also important to make sure that the solutions will last. The benefits of an initiative last even after the initial work is done.This could mean building roads, schools, or other public works, or helping the economy grow. These long-term fixes keep the same problems from happening again.The quote makes it clear that the best way to be effective is to combine both parts. Helping people today should also make things better in the future.The most important part of the quote is the idea of working at the "intersection" of urgent need and lasting impact. This means that short-term and long-term planning can meet at this point.People make choices at this intersection that take into account both the present and the future. For instance, giving immediate medical care is important during a health crisis. Investing in healthcare infrastructure also makes it easier to deal with problems that may come up in the future.This combined approach makes giving more useful and meaningful.The quote is from a leader in global business, but its message can be used by anyone. A lot of people want to help society, and this method gives them a practical way to do so.You can help someone right away, like by supporting local community projects, and you can also do things that will be useful in the long run. This could mean helping with efforts to protect the environment, improve education, or learn new skills.The goal is to think about both the short-term and long-term effects of what you do.Philanthropy is becoming more organised and planned these days. More and more, groups and people are focusing on results that can be measured and long-term sustainability.This quote fits with what's going on right now. It makes people think about the effects of their actions in the short and long term before they do anything. This method makes sure that resources are used wisely and that work actually makes a difference.Poverty, climate change, and public health are all problems that affect the whole world and need both short- and long-term fixes. No one answer will fix all of these problems.The quote shows that we need to find a middle ground. Meeting urgent needs and thinking about the long-term effects can help you come up with solutions that work and last.This balanced approach is being used by more and more businesses and governments around the world.Jeff Bezos's quote gives a clear and helpful view of how to give to charity. It stresses the importance of doing something now while also thinking about the future. It shows a way that is both responsive and long-lasting by focusing on the point where urgent need and lasting impact meet.This method lets us make real changes in a world where problems are always changing and getting more complicated. It shows that helping people now and making the future better are not separate goals, but things that can work together to make a difference.
Article Summary: Jeff Bezos is the founder of Amazon and the richest man in the world. His philanthropic work focuses on the intersection of urgent need and lasting impact. The quote shows that good philanthropy should do two things: act quickly and plan for the future. It shows that both short-term and long-term goals are equally important.
